title: Local Setup Toolchain
content_html:
Local Setup Toolchain

Current repo/runtime summary

Current baseline runtime:

  • Python: 3.12.7
  • Python packages: install from requirements.txt
  • Node: 22.22.1 verified local baseline
  • npm: 10.9.4 verified local baseline
  • Node packages: install from each feature-local lockfile

Current external tools used by media workflows:

  • macOS sips
  • ffmpeg
  • heif-convert from libheif

Public preview serves the tracked site/ root, and deploy validation runs through site-tools/.

Use Runtime Dependencies as the dependency-role reference.

Fresh macOS install

Recommended order on a fresh macOS 24 system:

  1. Install Apple command line tools if they are not already present:
xcode-select --install
  1. Install Node and the non-Python command-line dependencies with Homebrew. The current package tree is verified with Node 22:
brew install node ffmpeg libheif
brew install --cask miniconda
  1. Initialize Conda for your shell, then open a new shell:
conda init zsh

If you use bash instead of the default macOS zsh, run:

conda init bash
  1. Create and activate a dedicated Python environment:
conda create -n dotlineform python=3.12.7
conda activate dotlineform
  1. Install checked-in Python dependencies:
python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
  1. Install the locked Mermaid renderer dependency:
npm ci --prefix docs-viewer/build/mermaid

This installs only beneath that package root. Do not install mmdc globally.

Version checks

Check that the active Python is the expected one:

which python3
python3 -V
python3 -m pip check

Expected current value:

  • python3 -V -> Python 3.12.7

Check the media tools:

ffmpeg -version | head -n 1
heif-convert --version
sips --help | head -n 1

Check Node and the Mermaid package root:

node --version
npm --version
npm ls --prefix docs-viewer/build/mermaid @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli

Expected current values are Node v22.22.1, npm 10.9.4, and Mermaid CLI 11.16.0.

Check the public static site validator:

bin/site-validate

Switching to the correct Python

Activate the repo environment:

conda activate dotlineform

See available Conda environments:

conda info --envs

If python3 still points somewhere unexpected, check:

which python3
python3 -V

Codex runs should prefer the pinned local interpreter path:

$HOME/miniconda3/bin/python3
